Vertically within this container, the heading block occupies ~15–20% of the first screenful under the hero, the filter/sort band another ~15–20%, while the results area (active filters, list, pagination) fills the remaining vertical space and extends with scrolling. Individual components respect the 8px spacing scale: 32–48px gaps between major sections, 16–24px padding inside cards and bars.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout maintains a moderately spacious, editorial feel. The filter/sort band is visually dense but not cramped, with clear control grouping and breathing room. The active filters summary is a light, low-height strip. Dentist cards are large, card-based rows with generous padding, clear visual hierarchy, and comfortable vertical separation (24–32px) between cards. Overall, content is easy to skim, with enough white space to prevent the search experience from feeling like a cluttered directory.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow adapts the mockup’s Z-pattern to a search/results context. Under the hero, the eye first lands on the center-aligned 'Our Dentists' heading, then moves slightly down to the filter/sort band. Within that band, attention starts on the left-side core filters (specialty/search), travels horizontally to the right-side sort controls and child/schedule toggles, then drops down into the active filters summary strip on the left and continues vertically through the list of dentist cards. The container-aligned layout, strong horizontal filter band, and right-aligned sort controls create a clear series of horizontal anchor lines that guide scanning from left to right, then down, mirroring the mockup’s header/nav/feature band behavior.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s global hero and row of circular service cards, the 'Our Dentists' page content begins within a centered, 12-column container. A large, center-aligned H1 and brief welcome sentence mirror the spacious, editorial feel of the mockup, occupying the upper part of the main content area with generous vertical padding. Immediately beneath, a horizontal filter and sort band stretches across roughly 70% of the viewport, centered within the container. On the left, broader filters (like specialty and name search) sit in a lightly elevated bar; on the right, child-friendliness and Saturday availability toggles are balanced with a sort dropdown, forming a clear Z-shaped scan path. Below this, a slim active-filters strip spans the width of the content column, showing removable filter chips when relevant. The main body is a vertical list of dentist cards aligned in a central column that uses about 8 out of 12 grid columns, with wide side gutters. Each card is a white, shadowed panel with a circular dentist photo on the left (echoing the circular service icons above) and text details on the right, separated by comfortable padding and vertical spacing. When no matches are found, a centered, friendly message replaces the cards in that same column. At the bottom of the list, a center-aligned pagination row or 'Load more' button anchors the page. Throughout, the strong teal accents, subtle drop shadows, and generous spacing keep the layout feeling trustworthy, slightly traditional, and easy to navigate.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The URL state manager is a non-visual, structural component that conceptually spans the entire page. It sits at the highest logical level of the page layout, above the filter/sort band and dentist list, but does not occupy any visible space or alter the visual flow. All visible components within the main content container (filters, sort controls, active filters summary, results list, pagination) are clustered beneath the hero as described, while this manager works behind the scenes to keep the filter and sort state in sync with the URL.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered; no visual footprint; logically wraps or underpins the main search/filter area without affecting physical layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The filter options loader is conceptually positioned just above the visible filter and sort band, within the same central content container beneath the page heading. It does not present a persistent UI. If a loading state is needed, it can reserve a slim vertical slot directly where the filter bar will appear—approximately the same width as the filter bar (about 70% of the viewport, centered) and about 32–48px tall—before collapsing once options are loaded. In steady state, it occupies no space and the dentist-filter-bar and child-and-schedule-filters sit directly under the heading block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual in steady state; optionally overlays or temporarily occupies the filter band area with a subtle inline loader before yielding that space to the filter bar.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist filter bar forms the left and central portion of a prominent horizontal band directly under the page heading, echoing the strong horizontal nav/feature bands from the mockup. It is centered within the main content container and spans roughly 8–9 of the 12 grid columns (about 65–75% of viewport width on large screens), with equal side margins. Vertically, it is a shallow, pill-like card: 72–96px tall, with 16–24px of internal padding and a subtle drop shadow. The top of the bar sits about 32–40px below the page heading block, preserving the generous vertical spacing from the mockup. Within the bar, primary filters (e.g., specialty dropdown, dentist name search) are arranged horizontally from left to middle, leaving room on the right for the child-and-schedule-filters and sort-controls to align in the same visual band on larger screens. The bar anchors the start of the interactive experience, creating a clear, teal-accented focal line across the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content flow by default; optionally, on desktop, it can participate in a sticky filter band that pins the entire filter/sort row to the top of the viewport just beneath the main site navigation when the user scrolls past the heading.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The child-and-schedule-filters (such as toggles for child-friendly care and Saturday availability) are grouped on the right side of the same horizontal band as the dentist-filter-bar, visually balancing the denser left-side filters. On wide screens, they sit flush-right within the central container, occupying approximately the rightmost 3–4 grid columns and aligning vertically with the filter bar so that all controls form a unified strip about 72–96px tall. A small 16–24px horizontal gap separates these toggles from the left-hand filter controls, maintaining breathing room. On narrower screens, they naturally wrap to a second row directly below the main filter controls but remain within the same card-like band, still centered in the content column. The grouping is compact but well-spaced, with each toggle having 8–16px internal padding and clear labels, matching the moderately spacious density of the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows as part of the shared filter/sort band. On desktop, if the band is made sticky, these controls also remain fixed just under the main navigation as the page scrolls, ensuring quick access.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sort-controls sit in close proximity to the child-and-schedule-filters on the right side of the horizontal filter band, acting as the terminal point of the user’s left-to-right scan within that band. On large screens, the sort dropdown or segmented control is aligned to the far right edge of the content container, with the child-and-schedule-filters immediately to its left, together occupying about the right 3–4 columns of the 12-column grid. Vertically, the sort controls are centered within the 72–96px tall band so labels and icons align visually with the filter inputs on the left. On medium and small screens, the sort controls stack directly beneath or above the child-and-schedule-filters, still within the same card, maintaining a clear grouping. Below the band, a secondary instance of the sort-controls does not appear; the primary sort control remains the single sorting anchor, keeping the layout uncluttered.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows within the filter band; if the band is sticky, the sort controls remain visible at the top of the viewport, aligning with the right side of the content container as the user scrolls.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The active-filters-summary appears immediately below the horizontal filter/sort band, within the same central content column, acting as a thin, lightweight strip that visually separates the controls from the results. It spans the full width of the content column (matching the width of the filter bar above and the dentist list below) and is horizontally centered in the page. The strip is relatively low in height—about 40–56px—with 8–16px of vertical padding and 16–24px of horizontal padding inside. Active filter chips or tags are left-aligned within this strip, beginning near the left edge of the content column, while an optional 'Clear all' action sits toward the right. There is a clear 16–24px vertical gap between the bottom edge of the filter band and the top edge of this summary, and another 16–24px gap between this summary and the first dentist card, preserving the mockup’s tendency for generous separation between functional rows. When no filters are active, this strip collapses entirely, and the dentist list moves up to sit closer under the filter band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content flow; appears and disappears dynamically depending on filter state, but does not alter the overall container width or alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist-results-loader is associated with the dentist list area and is conceptually positioned just above the first dentist card within the same central column. It does not permanently occupy space. During loading states, it may temporarily overlay or sit in place of the dentist list as a centered spinner or skeleton placeholder region, matching the width of the dentist list (about 60–70% of viewport width, centered) and roughly one to two card-heights tall, while preserving the top and bottom margins that separate it from the active filters summary above and pagination below. Once data has loaded, it collapses fully, revealing the dentist-list or no-results-message.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual in steady state; optionally overlays or temporarily replaces the dentist-list region, remaining centered in the content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist-list is a vertically scrolling stack of dentist-card components contained within the central content column beneath the active-filters-summary (or directly beneath the filter band if no summary is present). It aligns with the same 12-column grid as the rest of the page and typically spans 8 columns in the middle (roughly 60–70% of viewport width), leaving 2-column gutters on either side for comfortable margins. The top of the list is separated from the section above by about 24–32px of vertical space, consistent with the mockup’s section spacing. Each dentist-card fills the full width of this column, and cards are arranged one per row with 24–32px vertical gaps between them. The list grows downward with content, with the bottom edge sitting about 32–48px above the pagination-or-load-more component, creating a visually distinct end to the list before the pagination controls. The overall impression is that of a clean, editorial directory—spacious, easy to scan, and clearly organized.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrollable with the page; its height is determined by the number of dentist-card items. It does not fix or float, but provides the main vertical content for the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each dentist-card is a horizontally oriented, white card with subtle drop shadow and rounded corners, filling the full width of the dentist-list column. It typically occupies the equivalent of all 8 central grid columns (about 60–70% of viewport width on desktop) with 16–24px internal padding on all sides. The card height is moderate—roughly 160–220px—depending on content length. On the left third of the card, a circular dentist portrait or placeholder image echoes the circular service icons from the hero area, reinforcing visual continuity. The remaining two-thirds on the right contain the dentist’s name (in a slightly larger, possibly serif heading), specialty, rating, location, and tag-like indicators for child-friendliness and Saturday availability, arranged in a tidy vertical stack with 4–8px gaps between lines. Action buttons such as 'View Profile' or 'Book Appointment' sit along the bottom right of the card, aligned horizontally with 8–16px vertical padding from the card edge. Cards are separated from each other vertically by 24–32px of whitespace, making the list feel airy and approachable rather than dense.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the list; each card maintains consistent width and padding. On hover (desktop), it may slightly elevate or intensify its shadow without changing its position or affecting surrounding cards.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The no-results-message appears in place of the dentist-list when filters yield no matches. It is centered within the same central content column beneath the filter/sort band and active-filters-summary. The message block spans about 50–60% of the container width, narrower than the full list to feel more focused and conversational, and is horizontally centered. Vertically, it sits with a 24–32px gap below the active-filters-summary and maintains at least 48–64px of whitespace below before reaching the pagination area (if present), keeping the layout feeling open despite lack of results. The message itself may include a small, friendly icon at the top, a bold line of text, and a slightly smaller explanatory paragraph beneath, all center-aligned. Optional guidance (e.g., 'Adjust your filters') can appear as a subtle link or button just below. The overall feel is calm and supportive, fitting the trustworthy medical aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally replaces the dentist-list region; static and centered within the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination-or-load-more component sits at the very bottom of the main results area, directly beneath the dentist-list or no-results-message, still within the central content container. It is horizontally centered and spans only as wide as necessary for its controls—typically 30–50% of the content width for a row of page numbers, or a single 'Load more dentists' button about as wide as a standard primary button. There is a clear 32–48px vertical gap between the last dentist-card and this component, reinforcing that the list has ended before additional navigation options appear. When using numbered pagination, page numbers are arranged in a single horizontal row with 8–16px horizontal gaps and generous tappable padding, matching the mockup’s button spacing. When using a load-more pattern, the button is slightly larger, reflecting its importance, and may mirror the style of the 'Request an Appointment' CTA from the header for visual consistency.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the end of the results list; moves downward as more results are loaded in a 'load more' scenario. Does not stick or float.

name: Dentist Profile
title: Dentist Profile
description: Displays detailed information for a selected dentist, including qualifications, specialties, locations, office hours (with Saturday availability), ratings, and a direct scheduling entry point with the dentist pre-selected.

core_features: Load and display a specific dentist’s profile based on the dentistId URL parameter using getDentistProfile.
Show dentist details including full name, biography (if available), specialties, pediatric status, and whether they accept children within specific age ranges.
Display an aggregated rating summary (star score and rating count) for the dentist.
List the dentist’s primary and additional office locations with basic contact details and link-outs to the Locations page, passing the relevant location identifier as a URL parameter.
Present office hours for each location and day of week, with clear indication of Saturday and evening availability based on office_hours and dentist.offers_saturday_hours.

Provide a clear Saturday availability indicator that confirms if the dentist offers Saturday appointments and at which location(s), using dentist.offers_saturday_hours, dentist.saturday_location_id, and office_hours.
Expose a prominent “Schedule with this provider” control that navigates to the appointment booking page with the current dentist pre-selected via URL parameters.
Handle loading, missing dentistId, and invalid or inactive dentist cases with functional error messaging and navigation guidance.
Expose pediatric-visit suitability (if the dentist is a pediatric specialist or accepts children under 12) to help parents decide if this dentist is appropriate for their child.

user_workflows.name: View dentist profile and confirm Saturday availability
steps: User navigates to dentist_profile.html with a dentistId query parameter in the URL.
On page initialization, the dentist-profile-loader component reads dentistId from the URL.
If dentistId is present, dentist-profile-loader calls getDentistProfile(dentistId).
While data is loading, the page shows a loading state and hides all dentist-specific components.
After a successful response, dentist-profile-loader distributes the dentist, primary_location, other_locations, rating_summary, and office_hours data to dependent components.
The saturday-availability-indicator component inspects dentist.offers_saturday_hours and office_hours entries where has_saturday is true, and cross-checks dentist.saturday_location_id if provided.
The component then displays whether Saturday appointments are available with this dentist and at which location(s), so the user can confirm Saturday availability.

user_workflows.name: Schedule an appointment with the selected dentist
steps: User arrives on dentist_profile.html with dentistId in the URL and waits for the profile to load via getDentistProfile.
Once the profile is loaded and dentist.active is confirmed, the schedule-with-provider-button component becomes enabled.
User clicks the “Schedule with this provider” button.
The schedule-with-provider-button component constructs a URL for appointment_booking.html with URL parameters sourcePage=dentist_profile and dentistId set to the currently loaded dentist.id.
The browser navigates to appointment_booking.html using that URL, allowing the booking page to pre-select the dentist based on the dentistId parameter.

user_workflows.name: Check where the dentist practices and navigate to a location
steps: User opens dentist_profile.html with a valid dentistId and waits for getDentistProfile to return data.
The location-list component receives primary_location and other_locations from the loader.
The component displays a list of locations where the dentist practices, including primary and additional locations.
User clicks a location entry in the list.
The location-list component navigates to locations.html with the highlightLocationId URL parameter set to the clicked Location.id (for primary location, this matches dentist.primary_location_id).
The Locations page can then use highlightLocationId to emphasize the chosen office.

user_workflows.name: Evaluate if the dentist is suitable for a child patient
steps: User lands on dentist_profile.html with dentistId and waits for getDentistProfile to load.
The pediatric-acceptance-summary component reads dentist.is_pediatric_specialist, dentist.accepts_children_under_12, dentist.min_child_age, and dentist.max_child_age from the loaded data.
The component presents whether the dentist is a pediatric specialist and any child age-range constraints.
User uses this information to decide whether to proceed with scheduling for their child or to return to the dentist list for a different provider.

user_workflows.name: Handle missing or invalid dentistId
steps: User attempts to open dentist_profile.html without a dentistId parameter or with an invalid dentistId value.
On initialization, dentist-profile-loader checks for the presence of dentistId in the URL.

If dentistId is missing, the loader does not call getDentistProfile and instead triggers an error state displayed by the dentist-profile-error-state component, suggesting navigation back to the dentist list or home page.
If dentistId is present but getDentistProfile returns an error or a dentist with active=false, the loader sets an appropriate error state.
The dentist-profile-error-state component then displays an error message and offers a navigation option (e.g., link to dentist_list.html) without showing any of the normal profile content.

interactions.trigger: Page load with URL containing dentistId parameter
interactions.action: dentist-profile-loader reads dentistId from query parameters and invokes getDentistProfile(dentistId).
interactions.result: Dentist profile data (dentist, primary_location, other_locations, rating_summary, office_hours) is loaded and propagated to child components for rendering.

interactions.action: dentist-profile-loader detects missing dentistId and skips calling getDentistProfile, instead setting an error or missing-parameter state.
interactions.result: dentist-profile-error-state component displays a functional error message and shows navigation options to go back to index.html or dentist_list.html.

interactions.action: dentist-profile-loader clears loading state and stores returned data in in-memory page state.
interactions.result: Header, rating, pediatric acceptance, locations, office hours, Saturday indicator, and schedule button components render based on the retrieved data.

interactions.trigger: getDentistProfile error (e.g., dentist not found or inactive)
interactions.action: dentist-profile-loader sets an error state that includes an error type (not_found or inactive).
interactions.result: dentist-profile-error-state component displays an appropriate message and hides all detail components.

interactions.trigger: User clicks a location entry in the location-list component
interactions.action: location-list constructs a URL to locations.html with highlightLocationId set to the clicked Location.id and updates window.location to that URL.
interactions.result: User is navigated to the Locations page, which can highlight the specified location.

interactions.trigger: User clicks the “Schedule with this provider” button
interactions.action: schedule-with-provider-button reads the current dentist.id from page state and navigates to appointment_booking.html with sourcePage=dentist_profile and dentistId set in the query string.
interactions.result: User lands on the appointment booking page with the dentist pre-selected using URL parameters.

interactions.action: saturday-availability-indicator evaluates dentist.offers_saturday_hours, dentist.saturday_location_id, and office_hours entries where has_saturday is true.
interactions.result: Component displays a clear indication of whether Saturday appointments are offered by this dentist and, if so, at which locations and during what office hours.

interactions.action: pediatric-acceptance-summary reads dentist.is_pediatric_specialist, dentist.accepts_children_under_12, dentist.min_child_age, and dentist.max_child_age.
interactions.result: User sees whether the dentist is appropriate for child patients and the applicable age ranges.

page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state is driven by the dentistId URL parameter: dentist_profile.html?dentistId={id}. On initial load, dentist-profile-loader reads dentistId from the query string. If dentistId is present, getDentistProfile(dentistId) is called once per load. If dentistId is missing, the page enters a missing-parameter error state. After a successful API response, the dentist, primary_location, other_locations, rating_summary, and office_hours objects form the in-memory page state used by all components. No persistent storage (such as localStorage) is used. Navigating away from the page (e.g., to appointment_booking.html or locations.html) passes state via URL parameters only: the Schedule button navigates with sourcePage=dentist_profile and dentistId=currentDentistId; location links navigate to locations.html with highlightLocationId set to the selected Location.id. Any re-visit to the Dentist Profile page requires dentistId in the URL to reconstruct state via a fresh getDentistProfile call.

components.functionality: Initializes the page by reading the dentistId URL parameter, invoking getDentistProfile(dentistId), managing loading and error states, and distributing the returned data (dentist, primary_location, other_locations, rating_summary, office_hours) to child components.
Displays functional error messages when dentistId is missing, getDentistProfile fails, or the dentist is inactive, and provides navigation links back to index.html or dentist_list.html.

Shows the dentist’s main identifying information, including full name, profile image (if provided), key specialties, pediatric specialist flag, and active/inactive status, based on the loaded dentist data.
Displays the dentist’s rating score and rating count as a read-only rating summary using rating_summary.rating and rating_summary.rating_count.

Indicates whether the dentist is a pediatric specialist and whether they accept child patients, including any defined minimum and maximum child ages using dentist.is_pediatric_specialist, dentist.accepts_children_under_12, dentist.min_child_age, and dentist.max_child_age.

Displays the dentist’s primary_location and other_locations, including their names and basic address/phone details, and exposes clickable entries that navigate to locations.html with highlightLocationId set to the selected Location.id.

Lists office hours grouped by location and day_of_week from the office_hours collection returned by getDentistProfile, highlighting which days have Saturday and evening availability based on has_saturday and has_evening flags.

Evaluates dentist.offers_saturday_hours, dentist.saturday_location_id, and office_hours entries with has_saturday=true to determine and display whether the dentist offers Saturday appointments and, if so, at which location(s) and during which hours.

Provides a prominent control to schedule an appointment specifically with the currently viewed dentist. When clicked, navigates to appointment_booking.html with sourcePage=dentist_profile and dentistId set to the currently loaded dentist.id in the query string. The button is disabled until the profile is successfully loaded and the dentist is active.
Displays the dentist’s biography text if dentist.biography is provided, or remains hidden if no biography is available.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content container (~1140–1200px). The row of four circular service cards suggests four equal-width columns on desktop.
layout.layout_pattern: The global page frame (hero image, logo, nav bar, and the row of four circular service cards) remains unchanged at the top. The Dentist Profile content begins directly beneath this hero band in the central content column. Components are arranged in a top-to-bottom stack, with a prominent profile header block at the top (dentist-header-summary, dentist-rating-summary, schedule-with-provider-button), followed by a thin information row (pediatric-acceptance-summary and saturday-availability-indicator), then a two-column information band (location-list on the left, office-hours-table on the right), and finally a more editorial biography area (dentist-biography-section) extending downward. Loader and error states temporarily replace the full Dentist Profile content area within this central column.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The global page frame (hero image, logo, nav bar, and the row of four circular service cards) remains unchanged at the top. The Dentist Profile content begins directly beneath this hero band in the central content column. Components are arranged in a top-to-bottom stack, with a prominent profile header block at the top (dentist-header-summary, dentist-rating-summary, schedule-with-provider-button), followed by a thin information row (pediatric-acceptance-summary and saturday-availability-indicator), then a two-column information band (location-list on the left, office-hours-table on the right), and finally a more editorial biography area (dentist-biography-section) extending downward. Loader and error states temporarily replace the full Dentist Profile content area within this central column.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The Dentist Profile content sits inside a center-aligned container of about 1140–1200px max-width, using roughly 60–70% of the viewport width on large screens. The profile header band (header summary + rating + schedule CTA) occupies roughly the upper third of the Dentist Profile area, with generous top and bottom padding (~32–48px). The information band (pediatric acceptance, Saturday indicator, locations, office hours) occupies the middle third with moderate spacing (~24–32px between blocks). The biography section occupies the lower third with larger vertical breathing room and longer text. Major vertical separations between these bands follow the 32–48px spacing rhythm, while internal paddings within cards and buttons stay in the 12–24px range.
layout_strategies.content_density: Density is moderately spacious. The header region for the dentist feels visually rich—photo, name, credentials, rating, and a strong CTA—yet each element has room to breathe due to ample padding and clear grouping. The locations and office hours table are more information-dense but constrained within neatly separated cards and a clear two-column layout. The biography area reads like an editorial block: large serif heading, comfortable line length within the central column, and noticeable vertical gaps between paragraphs and subheadings. Overall, no area feels cramped; even data-heavy parts maintain legibility through whitespace and structured hierarchy.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow continues the mockup’s Z-pattern. The eye first lands on the dentist’s photo and name at the upper-left of the profile header card, then moves horizontally across to the rating summary and the prominent schedule-with-provider-button on the upper-right. From there, the gaze drops diagonally down toward the central row of quick facts (pediatric acceptance summary and Saturday availability indicator). The flow then moves left across to the locations list and right to the office hours table, before finally descending into the full-width biography section. The central alignment of the container and a consistent teal/green accent color for key CTAs act as horizontal anchors guiding the eye downward.

layout.overall_description: Immediately beneath the site’s full-width header, navigation bar, and band of four circular service cards, the Dentist Profile page content begins in a centered, max-width container. At the top of this container sits a prominent profile header card: a soft rectangular panel with a subtle shadow, where the dentist’s circular photo is aligned to the left and their name and credentials sit just to the right, all within the left two-thirds of the card. On the right side of this same band, the rating summary and a strong teal “Schedule with Dr. [Name]” button form a compact CTA cluster, echoing the top-right emphasis of the global hero. Below this header block, a thinner horizontal strip introduces quick facts: whether pediatric patients are accepted and a visually distinct Saturday availability indicator, aligned so they span the width in a balanced left-right arrangement. Further down, the layout resolves into a two-column information zone within the same central container: the list of practice locations occupies about two-thirds of the width on the left, while the office hours table fills the right third, with the Saturday badge echoed near the top of this hours card. Below this band, the page opens up into a full-width, text-forward biography section, where a centered serif heading introduces comfortably spaced paragraphs detailing the dentist’s background. Throughout, generous vertical margins, a consistent 12-column grid, and traditional medical/corporate styling bind the components into a calm, trustworthy profile view that feels coherent with the rest of the site’s hero-driven design.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist-profile-loader occupies the main Dentist Profile content area beneath the global header, nav, and circular cards band. It is centered within the same max-width container as the profile content, spanning roughly 60–70% of the viewport width, with wide side margins on large screens. Vertically, the loader is placed around the center of the remaining viewport height, with at least 64px of empty space above and below it to maintain the site’s airy feel. The loader itself appears as a small card or circular spinner about 20–25% of the container’s width, centered both horizontally and vertically within this area, while all other profile components are hidden. This keeps the user’s focus in the middle of the screen, consistent with the hero-centric, centered layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily replaces the entire Dentist Profile content stack within the central container; remains in normal document flow but is the only visible element in that region until data loads.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist-profile-error-state appears in the same position as the loader or main content: directly beneath the circular cards band, centered within the approximately 1140–1200px max-width container. It takes the form of a horizontally centered alert card spanning about 50–60% of the container width, with generous padding (24–32px) and a subtle shadow to distinguish it from the background. There is comfortable vertical spacing above and below the card (around 32–48px), leaving plenty of breathing room. Within the card, error messaging is left-aligned, but the overall card is centered, maintaining the site’s balance. All other Dentist Profile components are suppressed when this error state is visible, so the card becomes the clear focal point.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Replaces the Dentist Profile content stack within the central container; scrolls normally with the page and does not overlay the global header or nav.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist-header-summary is the first persistent component of the Dentist Profile content and sits just below the global circular cards band, separated by a vertical gap of roughly 48px. It is rendered as a wide, shallow card centered in the page, aligning with the site’s max-width container and spanning about 80–90% of that container, leaving soft margins to either side. The card has 24–32px of internal padding and a subtle drop shadow to echo the hero’s depth. Inside, on desktop, the content is arranged in a two-column layout: the left roughly two-thirds of the card show the dentist’s circular portrait aligned to the far left, with their name (large serif heading) and credentials placed immediately to the right of the photo, left-aligned and stacked vertically with comfortable 8–16px gaps. The right third of the card is reserved for the rating summary and schedule CTA cluster (separate components, but visually grouped here). Overall, this header band occupies about the upper quarter to upper third of the Dentist Profile scroll area, making it the anchor for the rest of the information. On narrower screens, this card collapses into a single column with the photo, name, and credentials centered at the top and the rating/CTA cluster stacking beneath.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sits in normal flow within the central container; adapts from a two-column internal layout on desktop to a single-column, centered layout on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist-rating-summary is visually coupled with the dentist-header-summary card, occupying the upper-right quadrant of that card. On desktop, it sits to the right of the dentist’s name and credentials, aligned within the rightmost third of the header card. It is top-aligned with the dentist’s name, with about 16px of horizontal separation from the text block, keeping the right side of the header visually active in the Z-pattern. The rating area may consist of a star rating row and a numeric score, arranged compactly so that it occupies roughly the top half of this right column, leaving room just beneath for the schedule-with-provider-button. There is modest internal spacing (8–16px) between rating elements, preserving clarity without feeling sparse. On smaller screens, when the header card reflows into a single column, the rating summary appears centered below the name and credentials, with a 16–24px gap separating it from the text above and the schedule button below, thus remaining near the top of the profile content but in a stacked arrangement.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows inside the dentist-header-summary card area, aligning to the right on desktop and stacking beneath the name/credentials on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pediatric-acceptance-summary sits directly beneath the dentist-header-summary card as part of a slim information row. It is aligned to the left side of the main content container, starting roughly 24–32px below the bottom edge of the header card. The block spans about half of the container width on desktop (roughly the left 50–60%), giving it enough room for a short heading and one or two lines of explanatory text. The content is left-aligned with a small icon or accent (if used) placed to the left, echoing the circular shapes seen in the hero’s service cards. The right side of this horizontal band is visually balanced by the saturday-availability-indicator. This info row is less tall than the header card, occupying only about 10–15% of the initial Dentist Profile viewport height, and maintains 16–24px of vertical padding within the block to keep it readable. On smaller screens, the pediatric-acceptance-summary stacks above the Saturday indicator, both blocks remaining centered within the container with 16px of vertical separation between them.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow block just below the header card; part of a two-element horizontal band on desktop, stacking to a vertical sequence on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location-list occupies the left side of a broader information band positioned below the quick facts row (pediatric acceptance and Saturday indicator). There is a vertical gap of about 32px separating this band from the row above. Within the central container, the location-list spans roughly two-thirds of the width (about 8 of 12 grid columns), aligned flush with the left margin of the pediatric-acceptance-summary. The list itself is presented in a card-like panel or clearly grouped block, with a section heading at the top, followed by a vertically stacked list of locations. Each location entry is separated by 16–24px of vertical space, with address lines and contact details left-aligned for scannability. The overall height of this block can extend into a significant portion of the viewport depending on the number of locations, but the consistent spacing and left alignment maintain a calm rhythm. To the right of this block sits the office-hours-table in the remaining third of the width, forming a clear two-column structure. On smaller screens, the location-list expands to full container width and appears above the office hours section, with a 24–32px gap between them.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in a responsive two-column layout with the office-hours-table on desktop, and becomes a full-width stacked block above it on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The office-hours-table sits to the right of the location-list within the same horizontal band, aligned at the top so their headings form a clean row. On desktop, it occupies approximately one-third of the container width (about 4 of 12 grid columns), anchored to the right side of the central column. The table appears within a compact card or outlined panel, with its heading at the top, the saturday-availability-indicator visually docked near the heading, and the weekly hours arranged in a clear vertical list below. Internal padding is 16–24px, giving each row of the schedule enough space to remain legible without feeling cramped. Vertically, the office-hours-table is roughly similar in height to the location-list for one or two locations; with more locations, the left side may extend lower, but the consistent top alignment preserves visual order. On smaller screens, this entire hours card becomes full width and stacks below the location-list with a generous 24–32px vertical gap.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Forms the right column of a two-column band on desktop (aligned to the right edge of the central container), and reflows to a full-width block beneath the location-list on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saturday-availability-indicator appears in two closely-related visual roles within the mid-page information area. Its primary placement is as a compact badge or pill-shaped indicator aligned to the right side of the quick facts row, directly opposite the pediatric-acceptance-summary. This primary indicator sits about 24–32px below the header card and spans roughly 25–30% of the container width, right-aligned, with its text centered inside the pill. It uses a stronger brand green/teal fill and white text to stand out, mirroring the emphasis of CTAs but at a smaller scale. A secondary echo of this status may appear at the top of the office-hours-table card, docked just to the right of the hours card heading or in its top-right corner, as a smaller badge reinforcing that Saturday availability is part of the hours shown. Both instances maintain 8–12px surrounding spacing so they don’t crowd nearby text. On smaller screens, the main Saturday indicator stacks beneath the pediatric acceptance block, centered, and within the office-hours-table it remains near the top-right of that card but aligns naturally with the narrower width.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Displayed as an inline badge within two parent contexts (the quick facts row and the office-hours-table header); remains aligned to the right in horizontal layouts and centered/upper-right in stacked mobile layouts.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The schedule-with-provider-button is embedded in the right side of the dentist-header-summary card, directly beneath the dentist-rating-summary, forming a strong CTA cluster. On desktop, it sits within the rightmost third of the card, right-aligned, with approximately 8–16px of vertical space between it and the rating summary above. The button is visually prominent: a wide pill or rounded-rectangle button in the brand’s primary green/teal, spanning about 70–80% of that right column’s width and tall enough (at least 40–48px) to feel comfortably tappable. There is at least 16px of clear space below the button before the bottom edge of the header card, ensuring it does not feel cramped. This placement uses the page’s Z-pattern effectively by drawing the eye from the dentist’s name on the left to the actionable CTA on the right. On smaller screens, the button shifts to a centered position beneath the rating summary within the same header card, spanning about 60–80% of the central column’s width with generous margins on both sides, and with 16–24px of vertical separation from the rating summary and subsequent content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sits in the normal flow inside the dentist-header-summary card, aligning to the right on desktop and centering on smaller screens; does not stick or float with scrolling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dentist-biography-section occupies the lower portion of the Dentist Profile content, beginning about 40–48px beneath the bottom of the locations/office-hours band. It spans the full width of the central content container (roughly 60–70% of the viewport on large screens), centered with equal left and right margins. At the top of this section, a large, centered serif heading introduces the biography, echoing the typographic treatment of the main site H1 but at a slightly smaller size. Below the heading, a welcome-style subheading or tagline may appear, also centered but lighter in weight, with about 8–16px of vertical spacing. The body copy is then left-aligned within the same width for readability, with line lengths kept to a comfortable measure and 16–24px line spacing. Paragraphs are separated by 16–24px vertical gaps, creating an editorial feel with plenty of breathing room. Overall, this section often occupies the lower 30–40% of the initial profile scroll, extending further downward as needed for longer biographies, and serves as the calm, content-rich conclusion to the page’s layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard full-width block within the central container; scrolls normally and remains purely text-forward, without sticky or floating behavior.

name: Orthodontics & Invisalign
title: Orthodontics & Invisalign
description: Page for viewing orthodontic treatment options, comparing Invisalign with traditional braces, and starting an Invisalign consultation booking flow with pre-filled parameters.

core_features: Load orthodontic overview content (intro text, comparison points, treatment options, featured Invisalign option) via getOrthodonticsOverview.
Display a textual overview of orthodontic care using the returned intro_text.
Render a structured comparison between Invisalign and traditional metal braces using comparison_points from getOrthodonticsOverview.
List all available orthodontic treatment options from treatment_options, including identifying and highlighting the featured Invisalign option using featured_invisalign_option_id.

Within the featured Invisalign information, dynamically highlight that treatment is nearly invisible and, when the data indicates average duration under or equal to a configured threshold (e.g., 24 months), display an accompanying duration note derived from average_duration_months without hardcoding values.

Provide a primary 'Schedule Invisalign Consultation' call-to-action that navigates to appointment_booking.html with URL parameters sourcePage=orthodontics_page, ageGroup=adult, and serviceId taken from the related_service_id of the featured Invisalign treatment option.
Expose a 'View orthodontic pricing' navigation link that routes to services_pricing.html with the URL parameter category=orthodontic.
Respond to an optional focus URL parameter (e.g., focus=invisalign) by automatically focusing or scrolling to the Invisalign comparison section and adjusting internal highlighting logic.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Learn about orthodontic options
1. User navigates to orthodontics.html (with no focus parameter).
2. The page calls getOrthodonticsOverview and waits for the response.
3. Once loaded, the intro section displays intro_text to give general context.
4. The comparison section renders each comparison_points entry, showing Invisalign vs traditional braces descriptions.
5. The treatment options list displays all treatment_options, clearly marking the featured Invisalign option.
6. The user can read detailed Invisalign information, including invisibility and average duration messaging derived from the featured Invisalign option.

Workflow: Arrive specifically interested in Invisalign
1. User clicks a link from index.html or services_pricing.html that points to orthodontics.html?focus=invisalign.
2. On load, the FocusParamController reads focus=invisalign from the URL.
3. The page calls getOrthodonticsOverview as normal and renders intro, comparison, and treatment options.
4. After content is ready, the FocusParamController instructs the page to scroll to or emphasize the Invisalign comparison subsection inside the comparison component.
5. The comparison section visually highlights Invisalign-related details (e.g., active state for Invisalign column/row) based on the focus state without hardcoding copy.
6. The user can then proceed to the Invisalign consultation CTA from the featured Invisalign section.

Workflow: Schedule an Invisalign consultation from the orthodontics page
1. User reviews Invisalign information in the featured Invisalign section.
2. The InvisalignHighlightsPanel identifies the featured Invisalign option using featured_invisalign_option_id and pulls its related_service_id.
3. The InvisalignConsultationCTAButton becomes enabled only when a valid related_service_id exists for a currently offered adult Invisalign option.
4. The user clicks 'Schedule Invisalign Consultation'.
5. The click handler composes a URL to appointment_booking.html with query parameters:
   - sourcePage=orthodontics_page
   - ageGroup=adult
   - serviceId=<related_service_id from the featured Invisalign OrthodonticTreatmentOption>
6. The browser navigates to the booking page with these pre-filled parameters, allowing the booking flow to pre-select the correct service and age group and record the source page.

Workflow: Navigate to orthodontic pricing
1. User is deciding whether to start treatment and wants to understand pricing.
2. User clicks the 'View orthodontic pricing' link displayed near the comparison or treatment options list.
3. The click handler constructs a URL to services_pricing.html?category=orthodontic.
4. Browser navigates to services_pricing.html with category=orthodontic, allowing that page to filter and display only orthodontic-related pricing options.
interactions: On initial page load, the data loader component invokes getOrthodonticsOverview with no parameters and manages loading and error states.

When getOrthodonticsOverview resolves successfully, intro_text is bound to the intro section, comparison_points to the comparison section, and treatment_options plus featured_invisalign_option_id to the treatment and Invisalign highlight sections.

When the focus query parameter is present (e.g., focus=invisalign), the focus controller component sets an internal focus state and instructs the comparison section to scroll into view and apply Invisalign-focused highlighting after data is loaded.

In the comparison section, users may click internal anchors or buttons (if implemented) to jump between specific comparison topics; these interactions can update the focus state and optionally update the URL query parameter (e.g., switching to focus=invisalign) using URL manipulation without reloading the page.

In the treatment options list, clicking an option (other than the featured Invisalign) may expand additional details for that option using the existing treatment_options data without any extra API calls.

The Invisalign highlights panel reads is_nearly_invisible and average_duration_months from the featured Invisalign treatment option and conditionally renders duration-related text only when average_duration_months meets the desired threshold logic, avoiding any hardcoded numbers.

Clicking the 'Schedule Invisalign Consultation' CTA button triggers navigation to appointment_booking.html with query parameters derived from the featured Invisalign treatment option data (related_service_id) and static identifiers for sourcePage and ageGroup that describe this page and adult patients.
Clicking the 'View orthodontic pricing' link triggers navigation to services_pricing.html with category=orthodontic, allowing cross-page filtering based solely on URL parameters.

If the getOrthodonticsOverview call fails, an error state is displayed by the data loader component, and interactive elements that depend on the missing data (such as the consultation CTA) are disabled or hidden until data is successfully reloaded.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The primary external state for the Orthodontics & Invisalign page is managed through URL query parameters and the response of getOrthodonticsOverview.

1) URL Parameters:
- focus (string): When present, this parameter influences which section should be emphasized after content loads. Example usage: orthodontics.html?focus=invisalign.
  - On initial load, the FocusParamController parses window.location.search to read the focus value.
  - The focus value is stored in in-memory page state (e.g., pageState.focusSection) but is not persisted to any localStorage or cookies.
  - If focus === 'invisalign', once getOrthodonticsOverview has successfully returned and the comparison section is rendered, the controller triggers a programmatic scroll or focus to the comparison component or the Invisalign subsection inside it.
  - Components such as the comparison section can use the focus value to apply conditional styling or expanded views (e.g., marking Invisalign as the primary column) without relying on any hardcoded assumptions.
  - When users interact with internal links that conceptually change focus (e.g., a 'Go to Invisalign details' link), the page updates the URL with the appropriate focus parameter using the History API (pushState/replaceState) so that the current state is shareable via URL.

2) Data-derived State:
- The only data source is getOrthodonticsOverview. The response provides:
  - intro_text (string)
  - comparison_points (array)
  - treatment_options (array of OrthodonticTreatmentOption)
  - featured_invisalign_option_id (string)
- After the response is stored in memory, derived state is calculated without hardcoded values, such as:
  - featuredInvisalignOption = treatment_options.find(option.id === featured_invisalign_option_id && option.treatment_type === 'invisalign' && option.is_currently_offered && option.is_for_adults).
  - invisalignIsHighlightable = featuredInvisalignOption && featuredInvisalignOption.is_nearly_invisible.
  - invisalignDurationHighlightEnabled = featuredInvisalignOption && typeof featuredInvisalignOption.average_duration_months === 'number' and meets the business rule for highlighting (e.g., less than or equal to a configured max duration). The actual value displayed comes directly from average_duration_months to avoid hardcoding.
  - invisalignConsultationServiceId = featuredInvisalignOption ? featuredInvisalignOption.related_service_id : undefined.

3) Navigation State:
- When the user clicks the Invisalign consultation CTA, the navigation URL is constructed at runtime as:
  appointment_booking.html?sourcePage=orthodontics_page&ageGroup=adult&serviceId=<invisalignConsultationServiceId>
  where invisalignConsultationServiceId is read from the featured Invisalign option.
- When the user clicks the orthodontic pricing link, the navigation URL is constructed as:
  services_pricing.html?category=orthodontic
- No localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies are used to manage or persist state; all cross-page behavior depends solely on URL parameters and server-side data from getOrthodonticsOverview.

components.functionality: Fetches the orthodontic overview data for the page using getOrthodonticsOverview, manages loading and error states, and exposes the response (intro_text, comparison_points, treatment_options, featured_invisalign_option_id) to child components.
event_handlers: onInit: call getOrthodonticsOverview() and store the result in page-level state.
onSuccess: propagate intro_text, comparison_points, treatment_options, and featured_invisalign_option_id to subscribing components.
onError: set an error flag that other components can use to render fallback messaging and disable dependent actions.
onRetryClick: re-invoke getOrthodonticsOverview when a user attempts to reload the data from an error state.

components.functionality: Reads and manages the focus URL parameter to control which section of the page should be emphasized (e.g., Invisalign comparison) and coordinates scrolling/highlighting once content is loaded.
event_handlers: onInit: parse the current URL query string for the 'focus' parameter and store it as pageState.focusSection.

onDataReady: when getOrthodonticsOverview data is available and main sections are rendered, if pageState.focusSection is set (e.g., 'invisalign'), programmatically scroll to the matching section anchor (such as the comparison component) and notify that component of the focus value.

onInternalFocusChange: when a user triggers an internal action that changes focus (e.g., clicking an internal Invisalign link), update pageState.focusSection and modify the URL query using pushState/replaceState so that focus is reflected in the URL without reloading.
onUrlChange: if the URL changes while staying on the page (e.g., via History API), re-parse the focus parameter and adjust in-page focus accordingly.

components.functionality: Displays the introductory orthodontics overview text fetched from getOrthodonticsOverview, giving users context before they view comparisons and options.
event_handlers: onDataUpdate: when intro_text changes in the data loader state, re-render the intro content.
onErrorChange: if the loader indicates an error, hide or replace intro content with an appropriate non-data-dependent message.

components.functionality: Renders a structured comparison between Invisalign and traditional metal braces using comparison_points, and responds to focus state (e.g., Invisalign-focused view when focus=invisalign).
event_handlers: onDataUpdate: when comparison_points are updated, rebuild the comparison view by iterating through each comparison_points item to display title, invisalign_description, and traditional_braces_description dynamically.

onFocusChange: when pageState.focusSection changes (for example to 'invisalign'), adjust internal state so Invisalign-related parts are emphasized (e.g., highlighted column/row) and optionally scroll the section into view.
onAnchorClick: if users click internal anchors or links to specific comparison topics, update pageState.focusSection where appropriate and notify FocusParamController to update the URL query.

components.functionality: Shows all orthodontic treatment options returned in treatment_options, identifies the featured Invisalign option, and allows users to inspect details for each option without additional API calls.
event_handlers: onDataUpdate: when treatment_options or featured_invisalign_option_id change, compute derived state such as featuredInvisalignOption and mark it as featured.
onOptionClick: when a user clicks a specific treatment option, expand or collapse a detail view using only the data from that option (description, suitability flags, etc.), without re-fetching data.

onFeaturedOptionResolve: once featuredInvisalignOption is determined, emit an event or callback to inform the InvisalignHighlightsPanel and InvisalignConsultationCTAButton components of the selected option.

components.functionality: Presents key information about the featured Invisalign treatment option, including its near-invisibility and average duration, highlighting duration advantages only when supported by the data.
event_handlers: onFeaturedInvisalignChange: when the featuredInvisalignOption is set or updated, recompute flags such as invisalignIsHighlightable (based on is_nearly_invisible) and invisalignDurationHighlightEnabled (based on average_duration_months and business rules).

onRender: display text that references invisalignIsHighlightable and uses the exact average_duration_months value from the data (when invisalignDurationHighlightEnabled is true), avoiding any hardcoded duration values.

onNoFeaturedInvisalign: if no suitable featured Invisalign option exists (e.g., not offered), hide this panel or replace it with a neutral informational message that does not assume Invisalign availability.

components.functionality: Provides the 'Schedule Invisalign Consultation' action. When clicked, navigates to the booking page with pre-filled parameters, using the featured Invisalign treatment option’s related_service_id.
event_handlers: onFeaturedInvisalignChange: when the featured Invisalign option is set, read its related_service_id and determine whether the CTA button should be enabled. If related_service_id is missing or the option is not eligible for adults, disable or hide the button.

onClick: if the button is enabled, construct a URL to appointment_booking.html with query parameters:
          - sourcePage=orthodontics_page
          - ageGroup=adult
          - serviceId=<featuredInvisalignOption.related_service_id>
        and navigate the browser to that URL.

onDisabledClick: prevent navigation and optionally show a non-data-dependent message if the serviceId is not available (e.g., because Invisalign is not currently offered), without introducing static Invisalign details.

components.functionality: Allows the user to navigate directly to the orthodontic pricing section of the services_pricing page using a URL parameter.
event_handlers: onClick: construct the URL services_pricing.html?category=orthodontic and navigate the browser there.
onKeyboardActivate: support keyboard activation (e.g., Enter key) to trigger the same navigation logic for accessibility.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content container (~1140–1200px). The row of four circular service cards suggests four equal-width columns on desktop.
layout.layout_pattern: All page-specific components sit within the existing site template, directly beneath the global header/hero band and row of circular service cards. Inside the 12-column, center-aligned grid with a max-width of ~1140–1200px, the orthodontics page uses a single primary central content column occupying about 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop, aligned to the center. Components are arranged in a linear, top-to-bottom flow: first the orthodontics intro, then a structured list of treatment options, followed by the orthodontic comparison, and finally a visually emphasized Invisalign highlights + consultation CTA area. Non-visual logic components (loader, focus controller) sit at the top of the DOM structure but do not alter the visual layout.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All page-specific components sit within the existing site template, directly beneath the global header/hero band and row of circular service cards. Inside the 12-column, center-aligned grid with a max-width of ~1140–1200px, the orthodontics page uses a single primary central content column occupying about 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop, aligned to the center. Components are arranged in a linear, top-to-bottom flow: first the orthodontics intro, then a structured list of treatment options, followed by the orthodontic comparison, and finally a visually emphasized Invisalign highlights + consultation CTA area. Non-visual logic components (loader, focus controller) sit at the top of the DOM structure but do not alter the visual layout.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header/nav/hero/circular cards (outside this spec) consume the upper ~40–50% of the initial viewport. Immediately below, the orthodontics intro section occupies another ~15–20% of the viewport height with generous vertical padding. The treatment options list and comparison section together take up most of the scrollable area, each given clear vertical separation (32–48px) to feel like distinct editorial sections. The Invisalign highlights panel and CTA band form a compact but visually strong block near the bottom of the main content column, spanning roughly 60–70% of the viewport width and ~15–20% of the viewport height. Left/right margins remain generous, maintaining the feeling of an airy, centered content column within the broader page width.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout maintains a moderately spacious, editorial feel: large headings, clearly separated sections, and generous vertical spacing (typically 32–48px between major blocks, ~16–24px inside sections). The intro section is light on text with plenty of whitespace, while the treatment options and comparison sections present more structured information but still avoid crowding—using line breaks, row spacing, and comfortable padding. The Invisalign highlights panel is visually dense (compact key points) but framed by ample whitespace so it reads as a prominent, digestible summary rather than a wall of text.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The page continues the mockup’s Z-pattern: the user’s eye arrives from the header/logo area at the center of the screen where the circular cards were, then drops naturally into the centrally aligned orthodontics intro heading. From there, the gaze flows down the central column through the treatment options and comparison. Within each section, content tends to be center-aligned or slightly left-aligned but still within the central column, reinforcing a vertical reading path. The Invisalign highlights panel creates a visual anchor toward the lower portion of the page with a subtle card treatment and brand green/teal accents; the strong Invisalign consultation CTA button stands out within or just beneath this panel, attracting the eye to the right or center as a final action point in the flow. Links like ‘Orthodontic Pricing’ appear adjacent to the CTA as a secondary but clearly available path.

layout.overall_description: Beneath the site’s full-width medical/corporate header, hero, and the row of four circular service cards, the Orthodontics & Invisalign page content begins in a centered column against a clean, light background. A generously spaced intro section with a large, centered serif heading and short welcome copy sets the tone, occupying the upper portion of the main content area with plenty of breathing room above and below. As the user scrolls, they move down a single, comfortable vertical column: first a neatly structured list of orthodontic treatment options presented as stacked, card-like rows; then a side-by-side style comparison section that clearly contrasts Invisalign and traditional braces within the same central width. Below this informational content, a prominent Invisalign highlights panel appears as a card with a subtle shadow and brand-colored accents, standing out as a key decision-support element. Just beneath or integrated into the bottom edge of this panel, a strong, right- or center-aligned ‘Request an Invisalign Consultation’ button provides a clear next step, accompanied by a quieter ‘View Orthodontic Pricing’ link. The whole page feels orderly and trustworthy, with each section clearly delineated by 32–48px of vertical spacing, consistent margins, and a visual rhythm that leads the eye naturally from headline, to details, to comparison, and finally to booking an appointment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader component is mounted at the very top of the page’s main content area, directly beneath the global hero and circular cards band in the DOM order but not visually rendered. It has no visible box, occupies effectively 0px of visible height, and does not introduce extra spacing above the orthodontics intro section. The primary content column (intro, options, comparison, highlights) flows as if this loader were not present, maintaining the clean, uninterrupted central column visual layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual utility component. It does not contribute to layout geometry; it neither scrolls nor sticks, and it occupies no intentional width or height in the rendered UI.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The focus parameter controller lives alongside the loader at the top of the main content hierarchy, conceptually just inside the central content container but without any rendered interface. It does not appear on screen as a box, panel, or control and therefore does not affect the perceived spacing between the global hero band and the orthodontics intro section. All visible sections (intro, options, comparison, Invisalign highlights, CTA) sit below it and align normally within the central grid.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual state controller. Invisible to the user, zero-height/width, and fully passive in terms of layout; it scrolls with the DOM but has no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The orthodontics intro section is the first visible page-specific block below the global header/hero and the row of circular service cards. It is centered within the max-width content container (~1140–1200px) and constrained to a central column that spans roughly 60–70% of the viewport width on large screens, with equal left and right margins. The top edge of this section sits about 32–48px below the bottom of the circular cards band, creating a clear visual transition from the header tier into the main content. Inside this block, a large, centered serif H1 such as ‘Orthodontics & Invisalign’ sits at the very top, with about 16–24px of space beneath it before a slightly smaller, center-aligned welcome subheading. Below the subheading, any brief introductory body text is left-aligned but still within the same central column, with 16–24px line spacing and generous margins to keep the density moderate. The bottom of the intro section has another 32–40px of white space before the next section begins, maintaining the editorial, breathable feel of the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level section within the central column. It scrolls normally with the page and does not have sticky or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The treatment options list sits directly beneath the intro section, separated by a vertical gap of roughly 32–40px. It is aligned to the same central column as the intro, occupying about 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop and centered within the max-width container. At the top of this section, an optional small heading like ‘Orthodontic Treatment Options’ can be left-aligned within the column to subtly shift the reading focus toward a left-justified content block, while still visually anchored in the center of the page overall. Below this heading, individual treatment options (e.g., metal braces, ceramic braces, Invisalign) are presented as vertically stacked cards or rows, each spanning nearly the full width of the central column with 16–24px of internal padding and a very light shadow or soft border echoing the circular cards style from the hero band. There is about 16–24px of vertical space between each option row. The overall height of this section may be around 25–35% of a typical viewport, depending on the number of options, but the generous spacing keeps it from feeling crowded. A 32–40px gap at the bottom separates this section from the following comparison section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard flowing section; it expands vertically as needed based on the number of options and scrolls normally with no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The orthodontic comparison section appears immediately below the treatment options list, with about 32–40px of white space separating them. It continues within the same central column and width (about 60–70% of the viewport, centered). At the top, a medium-weight heading like ‘Invisalign vs. Braces’ is either centered or slightly left-aligned within the column, sitting about 8–16px above a short explanatory line if needed. Beneath this heading, the comparison content is arranged in a two-column layout within the central column: the left half dedicated to ‘Traditional Braces’ and the right half to ‘Invisalign’. Each column occupies roughly 48–50% of the central column’s width, with a small 16–24px gutter between them. Rows of comparison points run horizontally across these two columns—each row occupying the full width of the comparison area, with one cell in the left column and one in the right. Each row has 8–16px of vertical padding, and rows are separated by subtle horizontal dividers or ample spacing to avoid a cramped tabular feel. The section’s overall vertical space is generous yet structured, likely 25–35% of the viewport height, depending on content length. About 40–48px of space below this section visually prepares the user for the more focused Invisalign highlights and CTA band that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A normal, scrollable section that adapts its height to content. It maintains the internal two-column structure only within its own bounds and does not stick or float relative to the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Invisalign highlights panel is a prominent card-like block positioned below the comparison section, separated by a 40–48px vertical gap that marks it as a key decision-support area. It remains within the same central max-width container but appears as a distinct card centered on the page, spanning roughly 60–70% of the viewport width. The panel has 24–32px of internal padding on all sides, a soft drop shadow, and/or a light tinted background using the brand’s green/teal palette to echo the circular icons from the hero area. At the top-left of the panel interior is a concise heading such as ‘Why Patients Love Invisalign’, aligned left to reinforce reading flow. Below the heading, 3–5 short bullet points or brief highlight blocks are stacked vertically, each separated by 8–12px of space, providing clear, high-impact reasons to choose Invisalign. The right side or lower portion of the panel interior is kept airy to visually accommodate the CTA button that will sit immediately below or at the bottom edge of this panel. The panel’s overall height is compact but visually weighty—roughly 15–20% of the viewport height, framed by ample whitespace above and below so it stands out as a primary focus area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A standard card within the central column that scrolls with the page. It does not pin to the viewport but is visually anchored by its distinctive background and shadow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Invisalign consultation CTA button is positioned directly associated with the Invisalign highlights panel, appearing either immediately inside the bottom edge of the panel or directly beneath it with only a small 16–24px gap, so that the panel and button read as a single CTA band. The button is horizontally centered within the same 60–70% width central column, but its label and visual weight (strong brand teal/green fill, white text, subtle drop shadow) give it high prominence. The button’s tap target size is generous: approximately 200–260px wide (or auto width based on text) and 44–52px tall with comfortable 12–16px internal vertical padding. There is about 16–24px of whitespace below the button before any secondary link, ensuring it doesn’t feel cramped. On desktop, the button forms a strong horizontal focal point near the lower third of the main content, clearly signaling the next step in the flow: starting an Invisalign consultation with pre-filled parameters.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard interactive element within the flow. It scrolls normally, is not sticky or floating, and aligns to the center of the central column while respecting the same max-width constraints.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The orthodontic pricing link is placed in close proximity to the Invisalign consultation CTA button as a secondary action. It appears directly beneath the button, centered within the same central column and aligned to the button’s horizontal centerline, with about 8–12px of vertical space between the button and the link text. The link is styled as inline text—likely in the brand teal color but with normal weight and no filled background—so it is clearly visible but does not compete with the primary CTA. The overall visual effect is a small, quiet line such as ‘View orthodontic pricing’ or ‘See full orthodontic fees’, giving users an optional informational path. The spacing below this link (around 32–40px) acts as a soft footer to the Invisalign CTA zone, before any additional page content or the global footer begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline text link within the central column that scrolls normally. It has no special fixed or floating behavior and relies on its proximity to the CTA button for contextual prominence.

name: Insurance & Payments
title: Insurance & Payments
description: Allows patients to review accepted insurance plans, compare preventive coverage side by side, and proceed to booking or billing questions with their insurance plan context carried via URL parameters.

core_features: Load all accepted insurance plans and their coverage details using getInsurancePlansOverview.
Interpret the optional highlightPlans URL parameter and/or highlighted_plan_names from the API to pre-highlight specific plans for comparison.
Display a filterable list of insurance plans with key coverage attributes (preventive, basic, major, orthodontic) and acceptance status.
Provide a side-by-side comparison view for a small set of user-selected or pre-highlighted plans, emphasizing preventive coverage for routine services such as cleanings.
Automatically compute and indicate which compared plan offers higher preventive coverage, and clearly show if coverage is tied or unavailable.
Allow users to select an insurance plan and navigate directly to the appointment_booking.html page with sourcePage and insurancePlanId URL parameters set.
Provide a persistent link to contact.html with URL parameters prefilled for insurance/payment-related questions.
Handle users who are unsure of their plan by allowing them to proceed to booking without a preselected plan or to submit a billing question.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Compare highlighted plans for preventive coverage:
1) User lands on insurance_payments.html, optionally with highlightPlans in the URL.
2) Page calls getInsurancePlansOverview and receives the list of plans and any highlighted_plan_names.
3) The controller component parses highlightPlans (if present) and matches its values against plan ids and names from the API.
4) Any matched plans (or, if none, plans from highlighted_plan_names) are marked as "highlighted" and added to the initial comparison set.
5) The plan list displays all plans, visually marking highlighted ones.
6) The comparison table displays coverage details for plans in the comparison set.
7) The preventive coverage summary component compares preventive_coverage_percent for the plans in the comparison set and indicates which plan(s) have the highest preventive coverage or that they are tied.
8) User may add or remove plans from the comparison set via controls on each plan row/card.
9) The comparison table and summary update in real time as the comparison set changes.

Workflow 2 – Choose a plan and book a cleaning with that plan:
1) User reviews the plan list and/or comparison table.
2) User identifies a preferred plan (e.g., the one with highest preventive coverage) and clicks a contextual booking button associated with that plan.
3) The event handler reads the plan_id from the clicked plan.
