onBeforePrint: Optional hook to adjust layout or apply a print-specific class to the document so the Terms content prints cleanly.
onAfterPrint: Optional hook to revert any temporary layout changes made for printing.

components.functionality: Shows user-friendly error information when the Terms of Use content cannot be loaded and offers a retry option.
event_handlers: onErrorStateChange: When hasError becomes true, display the banner with an appropriate generic message and optionally log details for diagnostics.
onClickRetry: When the user clicks the retry action, emit a retry event to the terms-page-controller, which calls legal-content-loader.onRetry.
onHide: When the loader successfully fetches data after an error, hide the banner automatically.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content container (~1140–1200px). The row of four circular service cards suggests four equal-width columns on desktop.
layout.layout_pattern: The page inherits the global header, hero, and navigation band from the mockup, with the Terms of Use content starting immediately below the row of circular hero cards. Within the main content area, everything is arranged in a single, centered column that occupies roughly 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop. At the top of this column, a compact utility band mirrors the header’s right-aligned CTAs by placing the last-updated text on the left and the print control on the right. Directly beneath this utility band sits a full-width table-of-contents card, followed by the main legal content viewer. A low-profile section navigator bar anchors the bottom of the legal content area, keeping the overall flow linear and vertical, consistent with the original mockup.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page inherits the global header, hero, and navigation band from the mockup, with the Terms of Use content starting immediately below the row of circular hero cards. Within the main content area, everything is arranged in a single, centered column that occupies roughly 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop. At the top of this column, a compact utility band mirrors the header’s right-aligned CTAs by placing the last-updated text on the left and the print control on the right. Directly beneath this utility band sits a full-width table-of-contents card, followed by the main legal content viewer. A low-profile section navigator bar anchors the bottom of the legal content area, keeping the overall flow linear and vertical, consistent with the original mockup.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global header, nav, and hero region (including the existing circular cards) still occupy the top ~40–50% of the initial viewport. The Terms-specific content then begins with 32–48px of vertical breathing room before the utility band. The central legal column is constrained to about 720–800px max width and centered, with generous side margins that echo the mockup’s editorial feel. Vertically, there is roughly 24–32px between the utility band and the TOC card, 32–40px between the TOC card and the main legal viewer, and 32–48px between large section headings inside the viewer. The section navigator at the bottom is compact in height (about 56–72px) but spans the full width of the content column.
layout_strategies.content_density: While legal content tends to be text-heavy, the layout uses the site’s moderately spacious, editorial rhythm to keep it readable. Headings, subheadings, and section breaks inside the legal viewer are given generous top margins (24–32px) to visually chunk the document. The TOC card and section navigator break up the wall of text into navigable segments, maintaining a light, structured feel rather than a dense legal block. Inline line-height is slightly loose to match the friendly, traditional aesthetic, and no more than 10–12 lines of body copy appear without a break, list, or heading on typical desktop heights.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow begins with the established site-wide Z-pattern: logo at top-left, main CTA cluster at top-right, then across the navigation bar and hero imagery. As the user scrolls into the Terms area, the eye lands first on the centered page heading (rendered inside the viewer) and the left-aligned last-updated indicator, then sweeps right to the print button, echoing the top-right CTA emphasis from the mockup. From there, the gaze drops to the TOC card in the center, which scans left-to-right across section links, and then continues downward into the main legal content. The section navigator at the bottom forms a subtle horizontal anchor line that catches attention when users reach the end of a section, inviting them to jump to the next or back to the top, thus preserving the top-to-bottom narrative while still providing efficient movement through the document.

layout.overall_description: Below the familiar hero, navigation bar, and circular service cards, the Terms of Use page transitions into a calm, editorial-style legal area centered on the screen. A single, medium-width column carries all legal elements, framed by generous left and right whitespace that keeps the page feeling open and trustworthy rather than cramped. At the top of this column, a slim utility row pairs understated meta information on the left with a more visually pronounced print button on the right, mirroring the header’s CTA cluster. Immediately beneath it, a softly bordered, lightly shadowed TOC card sits like a feature band, listing the main sections of the Terms and inviting quick scanning. The bulk of the page is occupied by the legal content viewer, where headings in a serif typeface and well-spaced paragraphs flow down the column with ample vertical separation. At the bottom of the legal content, a low, streamlined section navigator bar spans the width of the column, providing previous/next section controls and a ‘Back to top’ action. Overall, the layout feels linear and structured, combining the brand’s traditional medical look with clear, navigable scaffolding around an otherwise dense legal document.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The terms-page-controller acts as the structural wrapper for all legal-specific components and begins immediately below the global hero and circular cards band. It defines a centered content region that spans roughly 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop, capped at around 1140–1200px overall page width but with the inner legal column further constrained to about 720–800px. All other legal components (error banner, utility band with last-updated and print button, TOC navigation, content viewer, and section navigator) are stacked vertically inside this controller, following the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow. There is a consistent 32–48px top padding within this controller before the first visible legal element, aligning with the vertical breathing room seen between major sections in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as a static, full-height container within the main page flow, governing the central column alignment and spacing without visible UI of its own.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-content-loader is positioned at the top of the legal content stack, just inside the terms-page-controller and conceptually above the legal-content-viewer. When active (during data fetch), it manifests as a centered loading indicator within the same central column width as the viewer, appearing in the vertical space where the legal-content-viewer will eventually render. The loader occupies a modest vertical band (roughly 80–120px height) with generous top and bottom padding to avoid feeling cramped, and it pushes the rest of the content (including the TOC and section navigator) downward, preserving the linear structure. On successful load it collapses to zero height and becomes effectively invisible, leaving the spacing defined by the other components.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily occupies the viewer’s space when loading; otherwise collapses and does not affect layout. It scrolls with the page and is not sticky.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-content-viewer is the dominant element within the legal area, positioned centrally in the page beneath the legal-toc-navigation card, with 32–40px of vertical margin separating them. It spans the width of the legal content column, approximately 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop, constrained to about 720–800px and centered with equal whitespace on both sides. Inside the viewer, the Terms of Use title appears as a large, centered H1 at the top, followed by a slightly smaller, centered welcome or explanatory subheading, echoing the mockup’s editorial style. The body of the terms flows below these headings as left-aligned text with comfortable line spacing and 24–32px of vertical separation between major sections or headings, keeping the dense legal copy visually digestible. The bottom of the viewer ends 32–40px above the legal-section-navigator, maintaining clear separation between reading area and navigation controls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable content block within the main page flow; it grows vertically with the length of the legal document and drives the overall page scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-last-updated-indicator sits near the top of the legal area as part of a slim utility band, directly below the start of the terms-page-controller and above the legal-toc-navigation card. It is left-aligned within the central content column and occupies the full column width but uses only a single line or two of text on the left side, with ample empty space to the right. On wider screens, the right side of this same horizontal band is used for the legal-print-control, creating a left-right balance that mirrors the mockup’s top-right CTA cluster. Vertical padding around this indicator is modest (8–16px), and there is about 16–24px of vertical space below it before the TOC card begins, so it reads as a discrete meta-information row rather than a full section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline element within the central column; it scrolls away naturally with the content and does not stick to the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-toc-navigation appears directly beneath the last-updated/print utility band, separated by roughly 16–24px of vertical space, and is centered within the same legal content column. It takes the form of a full-width card or band (within the column) with a subtle drop shadow and a light background tint, visually echoing the hero’s circular cards but in a rectangular format. The card has generous internal padding (24–32px) and lists the main Terms sections in a vertical or two-column layout, depending on available width, with clear spacing between each link to maintain the page’s moderately spacious density. This TOC card occupies approximately 20–25% of the initial legal viewport height before scrolling (often 160–260px tall, depending on number of items), making it a prominent visual anchor between the header area and the main legal copy. It spans the same width as the viewer, preserving a clean, aligned central block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static card within the main column; it scrolls with the page and remains at the top of the legal content area once passed, without sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-section-navigator is positioned at the bottom of the legal content stack, directly underneath the legal-content-viewer with approximately 32–40px of vertical separation. It spans the full width of the central legal column as a low-profile horizontal bar, about 56–72px tall, with a subtle brand-colored background that visually ties back to the strong teal navigation bar but at a lighter intensity. Inside this bar, controls such as ‘Previous section’, ‘Back to top’, and ‘Next section’ are aligned left-to-right, with primary movement actions (Next/Back) emphasized using slightly stronger text weight or pill-like buttons, while still feeling more understated than the main print CTA. On desktop, the bar visually anchors the end of the document, creating a clear bottom edge to the content while providing a natural call to continue navigating through sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static in the document flow by default; it appears after the main content and scrolls with the page. It can optionally become sticky near the bottom of the viewport on long documents, but remains visually confined to the central column width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-print-control appears in the same horizontal row as the legal-last-updated-indicator, aligned to the far right edge of the central content column. It takes the form of a medium-sized button with 12–16px of horizontal padding and 8–12px of vertical padding, matching the brand’s primary CTA styling with a strong green/teal fill, white text, and a soft drop shadow. This placement creates a mini CTA cluster at the top-right of the legal area, echoing the hero’s right-aligned ‘Request an Appointment’ button while remaining appropriately scaled for a utility action. The button sits vertically centered within the utility band, with enough surrounding whitespace (at least 8px from the top and bottom of the row) so it doesn’t feel cramped against the text or the edges of the column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline control within the utility band; it scrolls with the rest of the content and does not remain fixed when the user scrolls down the legal text.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The legal-error-banner is positioned at the very top of the legal content region inside the terms-page-controller, directly below the global hero/nav and above all other legal components (including the last-updated indicator and TOC). When present, it spans the full width of the central content column and features a strong, high-contrast background color (e.g., red or amber) with an error icon and message text aligned to the left, following the site’s status banner style. The banner’s height is defined by generous padding (16–24px top and bottom), making it visually prominent without overwhelming the page, and it pushes all other legal content elements downward to maintain a clean, stacked hierarchy. There is approximately 24–32px of vertical space between the bottom of the banner and the next element (typically the utility band), matching the spacing between major sections seen in the mockup. If not active, the banner area collapses entirely, and the rest of the layout rises to fill the space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears as a static, block-level element within the central column when errors occur; it scrolls with the page and does not remain fixed at the top of the viewport.
